title: Chapter 4 — Naming as an API Contract
CHAPTER 4 —
NAMING AS AN API CONTRACT
segments.body_html:
Follow one variable name from Figma to CSS. The name is color/brand/primary. The value is #0066ff.

Chapter 4 — Naming as an API Contract — The Figma REST API returns this variable as a JSON object. The name property is the string color/brand/primary — exactly

beats.text: Follow one variable name from Figma to CSS. The name is color/brand/primary. The value is #0066ff. The Figma REST API returns this variable as a JSON object. The name property is the string color/brand/primary , exactly as the designer typed it. Nothing in the API interprets or transforms it. It arrives at the extraction script verbatim.

segments.label: 01 / 12 · HOOK

segments.section: WHY THE NAME IS THE CONTRACT
title: WHY THE NAME IS
THE CONTRACT.
segments.body_html:
Endpoint path — Figma variable name (color/brand/primary)
Breaking change — Name change that deletes the old identifier from generated output
Major version bump — Explicit deprecation period + consumer migration
Stable interface — Semantic token name (color/brand/primary) that survives a value change

beats.text: The word "contract" has a specific meaning in software: a stable interface between a producer and a consumer that both parties can depend on. A CSS custom property name is a contract between the token pipeline (producer) and the component stylesheet that references it (consumer). The component stylesheet writes var(--color-brand-primary). It does not care where that value comes from , whether it was defined in a hand-written CSS file or generated by a pipeline from a Figma variable.

segments.label: 02 / 12 · WHY THE NAME IS THE CONTRACT

title: THE THREE-TIER
HIERARCHY.
segments.body_html: primitive tokens→semantic tokens→component tokens

beats.text: Before specifying naming rules, there is a structural question to answer: what does the name's hierarchy actually represent? The design tokens community has converged on three tiers, and understanding the tiers is necessary to understand why the naming rules are what they are. The first tier is primitive tokens. These are raw values with names that describe what they are, not what they mean. color/palette/blue-500 is a primitive: it names a position in the color scale. The value is #0066ff.

segments.label: 03 / 12 · THE THREE-TIER HIERARCHY

title: THE CONVENTION
segments.body_html:
Spaces in segment — Button / hover
Non-ASCII character — 🎨 Brand Primary
Uppercase letters — BrandPrimary
Platform prefix in source — --color-brand-primary

beats.text: A naming convention is only as useful as its enforcement. I will give you the concrete rules first, then the enforcement code. Every Figma variable name must satisfy these conditions:

segments.label: 04 / 12 · THE CONVENTION

title: COMPONENT AND
LAYER NAMES
segments.body_html:
Variables are not the only names the pipeline consumes. Component names, style names, and export layer names all become

Component and Layer Names — Component names in Figma become documentation paths, Code Connect mappings, and AI agent context. The convention here di

beats.text: Variables are not the only names the pipeline consumes. Component names, style names, and export layer names all become identifiers downstream. Component names in Figma become documentation paths, Code Connect mappings, and AI agent context. The convention here differs from variable names because component names appear as display strings in the Figma layer panel , they are seen by designers in their natural working environment and need to be legible there. The convention is Category/Variant/State, title-cased, slash-separated, no spaces around slashes: Button/Primary/Default, Card/Product/With-Image, Input/Text/Error.

segments.label: 05 / 12 · COMPONENT AND LAYER NAMES

title: THE TRANSFORMATION CHAIN:
ONE NAME, FOUR OUTPUTS
segments.body_html:
CSS — --color-brand-primary
Swift — Color.colorBrandPrimary
Android XML — color_brand_primary
JavaScript — color.brand.primary

beats.text: I want to make the determinism concrete by tracing one name all the way through. Input: Figma variable color/brand/primary, value #0066ff.

segments.label: 06 / 12 · THE TRANSFORMATION CHAIN: ONE

title: MIGRATING A
MESSY FILE.
segments.body_html:
The more common situation is not building a naming system from scratch. It is inheriting a file with 300 variables, half

Migrating a Messy File — Step one: run the validator against the current fixture. Get the count and categorize the violations. More than 100 viol

beats.text: The more common situation is not building a naming system from scratch. It is inheriting a file with 300 variables, half of them named Color 1 through Color 47 and the other half named things like Brand Blue, Action Green, and Dark Gray Hover. The migration path is predictable, and there is a specific order that prevents alias breakage. Step one: run the validator against the current fixture. Get the count and categorize the violations.

segments.label: 07 / 12 · MIGRATING A MESSY FILE

title: WHAT THE CONVENTION
CANNOT FIX.
segments.body_html:
I want to be honest about the limits of naming discipline, because I have seen teams treat naming rules as a complete so

What the Convention Cannot Fix — The naming convention ensures that names are machine-readable and semantically descriptive. It does not ensure that the

beats.text: I want to be honest about the limits of naming discipline, because I have seen teams treat naming rules as a complete solution to token governance and discover the gaps expensively. The naming convention ensures that names are machine-readable and semantically descriptive. It does not ensure that the names are correct , that color/brand/primary actually represents the primary brand color and not an accent or an error state that was mislabeled at creation. Correctness is a semantic audit problem, not a naming problem.

segments.label: 08 / 12 · WHAT THE CONVENTION CANNOT FIX

title: THE HISTORY BEHIND
THE CONVENTION.
segments.body_html: LLM Exercises→Generate and examine.→Apply to known context.→Stress-test a specific claim.→Draft or audit a professional deliverable.

beats.text: Long before design tokens existed, the front-end community was learning the same lesson through CSS architecture. In 2009, Yandex engineers published the Block-Element-Modifier methodology , BEM. The insight was identical to what design tokens formalized a decade later: if you name HTML class attributes by semantic role rather than visual appearance, the name survives reskinning. .button--primary survives a color change. .blue-button does not. The class name is a contract between the HTML structure and the CSS rule that styles it. Change the visual representation; the contract holds.

segments.label: 09 / 12 · THE HISTORY BEHIND THE CONVENT

·Any failing checklist item = revise the prompt in Exercise 3 to add the missing constraint and re-run.
Any "contract-violation" finding = add a note to your project CLAUDE.md"AI rename proposals require human review against actual token usage before applying to figma-tools fixtures."
·Proposals marked "unverified" = open the Figma file and confirm the token's role before adding to the rename mapping.

beats.text: validate-names.js reads fixtures/variables.json and validates against naming.config.js. Never modify naming.config.js without explicit instruction , the approved category list is a team contract, not a configuration preference. What you're validating: The naming-audit table produced by Exercise 3 (preferred), or a pre-generated table provided below if you do not have a Figma fixture.
